summary: Proposes that AI developers produce a safety case — a structured rationale that an AI system is unlikely to cause a catastrophe — before training or deploying advanced systems. Following UK safety-engineering standards, the paper summarizes safety cases in Goal Structuring Notation (GSN), a flow-chart notation connecting claims (goals), justification strategies, and evidence. It organizes safety arguments into four categories: inability, control, trustworthiness, and deference to credible AI advisors. First posted March 15, 2024 (v2 March 18, 2024).
title: Safety Cases: How to Justify the Safety of Advanced AI Systems
